symptoms.symptom_name: Unprotected intercourse (barrier failure) approximately 12 hours ago

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Recent unprotected intercourse within the 120-hour window is the defining trigger for emergency contraception.

symptoms.symptom_name: Peri-ovulatory timing — day 14 of a regular 28-day cycle, self-tracked ovulation day

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Peri-ovulatory timing gives the highest conception risk and narrows the effective-agent choice (ulipristal acetate preferred over levonorgestrel once the LH surge begins) — the driver of the time-critical, same-day window.

symptoms.symptom_name: No nausea, vomiting, pelvic or abdominal pain

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Absence of pelvic pain and abnormal bleeding argues against an ectopic at this time (does not exclude a future one).
No pelvic pain, fever, or discharge.

symptoms.symptom_name: No liver-enzyme-inducing medications and no regular contraception

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Absence of enzyme-inducing drugs removes a factor that would reduce oral EC efficacy and change agent/dose selection — a pharmacology-check input relevant to the pharmacist/clinician choice.

symptoms.symptom_name: Acute situational anxiety / panic about unintended pregnancy

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Genuine distress, but non-specific and NOT a driver of triage tier — must not be mistaken for a marker of medical emergency.

investigation_details.expected_result: Negative (no already-established pregnancy from a prior cycle)
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: Cannot confirm the absence of an already-established pregnancy at the point of care; a confirmatory test at 3-4 weeks is still required regardless.

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A negative point-of-care test with a normal recent period makes an already-established pregnancy unlikely — a clinician-obtained result, not available over chat.

symptoms.symptom_name: Haemodynamically stable, soft non-tender abdomen on examination

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Stable vitals and a benign abdomen argue against an acute ectopic — a clinician examination the AI Doctor cannot perform; it infers stability from the absence of reported pain/faintness, not from a measured exam.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Confirmatory urine pregnancy test at 3-4 weeks
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Emergency contraception does not disrupt an established pregnancy; a follow-up test confirms it was successful.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: STI screening (chlamydia / gonorrhoea +/- others) per risk
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Unprotected intercourse carries STI risk; emergency contraception gives no STI protection, so screening should be offered.
